title: How the Pieces Fit Together
concept: Text encoder produces conditioning, U-Net denoises in latent space guided by that conditioning, VAE decoder produces the final image — three components, one pipeline. Boss explain ends with: 'Next up: Prompting Text-to-Image Models.'

exercises.prompt: When you follow a recipe, which order makes sense?

options.text: Read the recipe → cook the ingredients → plate the dish
Plate the dish → read the recipe → cook the ingredients
Cook the ingredients → plate the dish → read the recipe

exercises.explain: Just like a recipe has steps in order, an AI image generator has steps in order — understand the words, build the picture, then finish it up!

cards.title: One Bridge, Three Parts
cards.body: You've met the text encoder, the U-Net, and the VAE separately. But how do they actually work together to turn 'a cat wearing a hat' into a real picture? Today we build the whole bridge, start to finish!

cards.title: The Full Pipeline
cards.body: Three parts work as a team: 1) The TEXT ENCODER reads your words and turns them into a math 'meaning code' called conditioning. 2) The U-NET starts with random noise in latent space and slowly removes noise, guided by that conditioning, step by step. 3) The VAE DECODER takes the finished clean latent and unfolds it into the final, full-size image you see.

cards.title: Like Building a House
cards.body: The text encoder is the ARCHITECT who reads your wishes and draws a blueprint (conditioning). The U-Net is the CONSTRUCTION CREW who builds the house from a rough frame, following the blueprint carefully. The VAE decoder is the INTERIOR DESIGNER who takes the finished structure and makes it look real and beautiful for you to walk into.

cards.title: Follow a Dragon Prompt
cards.body: You type 'a blue dragon flying over mountains.' Step 1: The text encoder turns those words into a conditioning code. Step 2: The U-Net starts with pure noise and denoises it over many steps, using the code to steer it toward 'dragon' and 'mountains.' Step 3: The VAE decoder expands the finished clean latent into a full-color image of your dragon!

cards.title: Myth: One Part Does Everything
cards.body: Myth: A single AI 'brain' does all the work at once. Truth: It's a team effort! The text encoder never touches pixels, the U-Net never reads words directly, and the VAE decoder never sees your prompt at all. Each part has one job, and together they make the magic happen.

cards.title: Words vs. Pixels
cards.body: The pipeline moves from meaning to image in clear stages.

left.text: Turns your words into a conditioning code — no image yet, just meaning.

right.text: Turns a finished latent into the final picture — no words involved here.

exercises.prompt: What is the correct order of the three components in the pipeline?
exercises.explain: The text encoder makes the conditioning first, the U-Net denoises using it, and the VAE decoder turns the final latent into a real image last.

exercises.prompt: True or False: The U-Net denoises the latent while directly reading your text prompt letter by letter.
exercises.explain: False! The U-Net never reads raw text. It only uses the conditioning code that the text encoder already prepared.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete the pipeline description.
exercises.sentence: The U-Net denoises in latent space guided by the conditioning, and then the ___ turns the final latent into the finished image.
exercises.explain: The VAE decoder is the last step — it expands the clean latent into the full-size picture you actually see.

exercises.prompt: Which job belongs to the text encoder in the pipeline?

options.text: Turning words into a conditioning code that guides the image
Removing noise from the latent step by step
Expanding the latent into the final full-size image

exercises.explain: The text encoder's only job is reading meaning from words and creating the conditioning — it never touches pixels or noise directly.

exercises.prompt: A friend asks: 'How do these three AI parts actually work together to make a picture from my words?' What's the best explanation?

options.text: The text encoder turns your words into a conditioning code, the U-Net uses that code to denoise a latent step by step, and the VAE decoder expands the finished latent into the final image. Next up: Prompting Text-to-Image Models.
The VAE decoder reads your words first, then the U-Net makes noise, then the text encoder finishes the picture. Next up: Prompting Text-to-Image Models.
All three parts read your text prompt at the same time and each draws a piece of the final image. Next up: Prompting Text-to-Image Models.
The U-Net encodes your words while the text encoder removes noise, and the VAE decoder saves the file. Next up: Prompting Text-to-Image Models.

exercises.explain: Exactly! Text encoder → conditioning, U-Net → denoising guided by that conditioning, VAE decoder → final image. Three components, one smooth pipeline. Next up: Prompting Text-to-Image Models.
